message:
test(ft-xbnl0.2.4): POST mid-flight cx-cancel verb-parity (tick 389)

Added `distributed_http_client_post_mid_flight_cancel_returns_cancelled`
— POST variant of tick 380's mid-flight cancel test, verifying the
ft-l9mxa fix (tick 387) applies to POST not just GET.

Same structure as the GET test: server accepts + holds the stream so
the client stalls on response-read; concurrent task cancels cx 50 ms
after POST starts; assert elapsed < 1s + exact
Ok(Err(ClientError::Cancelled)) match.

Observed: elapsed = 74.5 ms — one 50 ms cancel-watcher poll cycle +
task scheduling. Same fast-cancel contract as GET (tick 380: 70 ms).

Why add a verb-parity test: the fix lives in `race_with_cx_cancel`
which both `::get` and `::post` delegate to. In principle both
inherit the cancel-watcher race. In practice, verifying empirically
catches any regression where a future refactor bypassed the race for
one verb but not the other (e.g. a per-verb optimization direct
`.post().await` that skips the race helper).

Verb-parity matrix for ft-l9mxa cancel-watcher coverage:
  - GET  mid-flight cancel returns Cancelled  — tick 380 (flipped tick 387)
  - POST mid-flight cancel returns Cancelled  — this tick

Test result: 1 passed; 0 failed
Compile time: 1m 18s cold.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
